metric_quality_table.description: Three different metric-quality observables, all bundled in this companion package. They probe complementary facets of the discrete-to-continuum bridge.

axes.name: Riemannian-embedding stress (spatial only)
axes.scope: Kruskal-Shepard MDS in 3-D Euclidean target dimension; signature-blind; bottoms out at sigma_inf ~ 0.30 under two-point Richardson, i.e. does NOT vanish at the available lattice sizes.

axes.name: Lorentzian light-cone fuzziness (3+1 causal-ordering)
axes.scope: Spectral-dimension residual of the 3+1 emergent-spacetime layer; the right 3+1-aware metric-quality complement to sigma(N).

axes.name: Einstein-identity gap
axes.scope: Direct test of the Einstein equations on the Lorentzian 3+1 manifold; converges under two-point Richardson at alpha = 0.848 to gap_inf ~ 1e-6.

differences.interpretation: sigma is roughly the sum of (i) a 3-D vs 3+1 dimension/signature-mismatch contribution, captured at canonical regime by sigma - f_LC ~ 0.11, and (ii) the 3+1-Lorentzian intrinsic light-cone fuzziness f_LC ~ 0.17. The Einstein-identity gap is in the same range as f_LC and converges, while sigma does not.
